Description: Central reactive state: step, source, uploaded image, selections, signals, decision, orientation, issuer, bankTemplate, embosserReady
Use Cases: UC: All modules read/write state for cross-module communication

Description: Blacklists for celebrities (13), brands (10), political, religious, weapons, unsafe content + REWRITE_MAP for safe alternatives
Use Cases: UC: Prompt contains 'iron man' → rewritten to 'armored warrior'. UC: Prompt with 'nike' → rewritten to 'athletic swoosh design'

Description: AU BANK template with SVG mock card front/back, chip cutout, brand plate, name/number plates, magstripe, signature strip, CVV regions
Use Cases: UC: Card art must preserve bank's embossable registration zones (chip, brand, name/number plates). UC: Different issuers can have different templates

Description: Canvas-based cover-fit composition: layers AI artwork onto bank template preserving DPI/dimensions, adds imperceptible registration marks
Use Cases: UC: AI image is composited onto bank card template at correct dimensions for physical embossing. UC: Orientation-aware — horizontal vs vertical cards compose differently

Description: 5 providers: Pollinations (free), Gemini (image-to-image), DALL-E 3, Grok, Stability AI. Metadata: label, needsKey, keyHint
Use Cases: UC: Ops team selects which AI model generates customer artwork. UC: Pollinations works without API key for demos

Description: Persist provider selection and API keys across browser sessions
Use Cases: UC: Ops configures Gemini API key → key persists on page reload. UC: Switching providers remembers previously entered keys

Description: Constructs generation prompt from style/mood/color/background selections + free-text. Adds 'luxury credit card art' and '4k detail' instructions
Use Cases: UC: User selects watercolor+calm+warm+mountains → prompt combines all into coherent generation instruction

Description: Image-to-image prompt for Gemini: aggressive wording to force heavy restyling while maintaining subject pose
Use Cases: UC: User uploads selfie → edit prompt instructs Gemini to restyle as 'watercolor calm warm' while keeping face

Description: Downsize uploaded images before API transmission via canvas. Configurable max dimension and JPEG quality
Use Cases: UC: User uploads 4000x3000 photo → resized to 1024px max dimension at 0.9 quality before sending to Gemini

Description: Free text-to-image API. Supports width/height for orientation, seed for reproducibility, no auth required
Use Cases: UC: Demo/sandbox usage without API keys. Vertical: 540x864, Horizontal: 864x540

Description: Gemini image generation with 3 model fallback candidates. Supports BOTH text-to-image AND image-to-image (edit). Aspect ratio: 9:16 / 16:9
Use Cases: UC: User uploads photo → Gemini edits it in selected style. UC: Model 1 fails → falls back to model 2 → model 3. UC: Safety block returns text instead of image → surfaced as error

Description: OpenAI DALL-E 3 text-to-image. Orientation via size param: 1024x1792 (vertical) / 1792x1024 (horizontal)
Use Cases: UC: Ops selects DALL-E → customer gets OpenAI-quality generations

Description: Grok-2-image text-to-image. No aspect ratio support — orientation accepted for parity but ignored
Use Cases: UC: Ops selects Grok → result may need cropping since aspect ratio not controllable

Description: Stable Diffusion 3 via FormData API. Aspect ratio: 9:16 / 16:9. Returns blob → objectURL
Use Cases: UC: Ops selects Stability → high-quality SD3 generations with correct orientation

Description: Routes prompt+image+orientation to selected provider. Validates API key requirement before calling
Use Cases: UC: callProvider() routes to correct provider based on settings. UC: Provider needs key but none configured → clear error message

Description: Fires N=3 parallel provider calls. Tolerates partial failures (Promise.all with catch). Each variation carries per-orientation cache
Use Cases: UC: 2 of 3 variations succeed → user sees 2 good + 1 failed. UC: All 3 fail → full error banner with per-variation messages. UC: Horizontal generated first → vertical slot stays null until toggled

Description: On cache miss when toggling orientation, re-calls provider with same prompt at new orientation. Shows re-rendering spinner
Use Cases: UC: User generates horizontal → toggles to vertical → cache miss → spinner → regenerates at 9:16 → caches result. UC: Toggle back to horizontal → cache hit → instant swap

Description: Render 3 thumbnail cards with click selection. Shows orientation-specific cached image. Updates live preview + embosser output on selection
Use Cases: UC: Customer clicks variation 2 → preview updates → embosser output recalculated. UC: Failed variation shows error state in thumbnail

Description: Shows current provider name + key status in customer sidebar
Use Cases: UC: Provider banner shows 'Google Gemini · Nano Banana ✓' or 'DALL-E 3 (key missing)'

Description: Switch between Customer Journey, Ops Dashboard, Architecture views. Auto-renders ops queue on switch
Use Cases: UC: Click 'Ops Dashboard' tab → ops view shows, customer view hides. UC: Click 'Customer Journey' → refreshes provider banner

Description: 4-step wizard: Source → Customize → AI Review → Result. Prev/Next buttons with validation gating
Use Cases: UC: Step 1 → must select source before Continue. UC: Step 2 → must select style. UC: Step 3 → auto-runs pipeline. UC: Step 4 → button says 'Start over'

Description: Two cards: Upload a photo / Generate with AI. Upload shows drop zone, generate auto-advances to step 2
Use Cases: UC: Click Upload → file drop zone appears. UC: Click Generate → upload zone hides. UC: Switch back to Upload → previously uploaded photo restores on preview

Description: Accept image uploads via click-to-browse or drag-drop. Validates type/size. Shows file metadata (name, size, dimensions)
Use Cases: UC: User drops JPG → validates → shows '2.4 MB · 3024 x 4032' metadata. UC: User drops PDF → rejected. UC: User drops 20MB file → rejected

Description: 4 chip-row groups (style/mood/color/background). Single-select per group. Updates state.selections[group]
Use Cases: UC: Click 'Cyberpunk' → style selected. Click 'Anime' → Cyberpunk deselected, Anime selected. UC: Each selection updates live preview

Description: Optional text field under 'Advanced' details accordion. Stored in state.freeText, updates preview
Use Cases: UC: User types 'cinematic hero under neon lights' → preview prompt updates → sanitized by L0 during pipeline

Description: Real-time card preview with CSS art classes + prompt text. Clears AI image when selections change to force regen
Use Cases: UC: Select 'watercolor' → card gets art-watercolor CSS class for placeholder visual. UC: Change mood → AI image clears so next pipeline run generates fresh

Description: Scan prompt for restricted keywords, rewrite violations, compute risk score (40–70 points per violation category)
Use Cases: UC: Prompt 'iron man style' → rewritten to 'armored warrior style', promptRisk=40. UC: Prompt 'nike logo' → rewritten, risk=50. UC: Clean prompt → risk=0

Description: Build pipeline UI for layers L0–L5. Each layer animates through pending→running→pass/warn/fail states with configurable delay
Use Cases: UC: User reaches step 3 → 6 layers animate sequentially → each shows pass/warn/fail → final result computed. Total animation ~3.8s hides AI generation latency

Description: File integrity, resolution (2048x1290), DPI (600), blur detection, steganography check (all simulated)
Use Cases: UC: Layer passes if file OK — simulated quality checks always pass in prototype

Description: Multi-model vision: NSFW %, face count, celebrity confidence, logo detection, OCR chars, CLIP similarity (simulated with random noise)
Use Cases: UC: Generates realistic-looking signal values so ops dashboard has data to display

Description: Weighted aggregation: nsfw(0.30) + celebrity(0.20) + promptRisk(0.15) + logo(0.10) + text(0.10) + objects(0.10) + noise(0.05)
Use Cases: UC: High NSFW → pushes risk score up. UC: Celebrity + prompt flags → combined risk. Outputs riskScore 0–100 and safetyScore

Description: Decision based on risk: AUTO_APPROVE (<20), QUICK_REVIEW (20–39), MANUAL_REVIEW (40–69), REJECTED (70+)
Use Cases: UC: Risk 15 → auto-approved, green card. UC: Risk 35 → quick review, yellow card. UC: Risk 75 → rejected, red card with block reason

Description: Behavioral signals: userRisk float + pHash duplicate detection (simulated)
Use Cases: UC: Monitors submission velocity and perceptual hash for bypass detection

Description: Display decision card (pass/warn/fail), risk meter bar, 9-signal grid, variation thumbnails. Enable/disable submit based on decision
Use Cases: UC: Auto-approved → green decision card + submit enabled. UC: Rejected → red card + submit disabled with explanation. UC: Manual review → submit enabled with warning

Description: Clear all state fields + UI elements and return to step 1
Use Cases: UC: User finishes flow → clicks 'Start over' → everything resets to initial state

Description: In-memory array with 12 pre-populated demo submissions. Each has id, cardholder, risk, safety, confidence, style, mood, flags, signals, art, imageUrl, orientation
Use Cases: UC: Ops dashboard shows realistic queue on first load without requiring customer submissions

Description: Generate tracking IDs in format CUST-XXXX (range 8000–9999)
Use Cases: UC: Each submission gets unique tracking ID shown in ops queue and confirmation

Description: Push to front of queue, remove by ID, update queue counter stat
Use Cases: UC: New submission → appears at top of ops queue. UC: Approve/reject → removed from queue

Description: Grid layout with risk-colored cards showing: thumbnail, name, ID, time, risk/safety/confidence scores, risk bar, cohort approval %, flags, signal breakdown toggle, approve/reject buttons
Use Cases: UC: Ops sees all pending submissions at a glance. UC: Click 'View signal breakdown' → expands detailed signals. UC: Vertical submissions render as portrait thumbnails

Description: Format signal data into labeled rows: Prompt Risk, NSFW, Faces, Celebrity, Logo, OCR, CLIP, User Risk
Use Cases: UC: Ops expands signal breakdown → sees exact scores for each moderation signal

Description: Move item to approved history, remove from queue, show success toast
Use Cases: UC: Ops clicks Approve → item moves to history → toast confirms → queue refreshes

Description: Rejection dialog with 5 preset reasons + free-text. Validates non-empty reason before rejecting
Use Cases: UC: Ops clicks Reject → modal with presets (Quality, Prohibited, Celebrity, Brand, Text) → select or type reason → confirm. UC: Empty reason → validation blocks

Description: In-memory approved[] and rejected[] arrays with timestamps
Use Cases: UC: All approve/reject actions create audit trail with timestamp

Description: Toggle panel with approved/rejected tabs, grid/list view toggle, item cards with decision tags and rejection reasons
Use Cases: UC: Ops clicks History → panel opens showing approved cards. UC: Switch to Rejected tab → shows rejected cards with reasons. UC: Toggle grid/list view

Description: Provider dropdown, API key input with show/hide toggle, Save and Test Connection buttons
Use Cases: UC: Ops selects Gemini → API key field appears. UC: Enters key → Save → persisted to localStorage. UC: Test Connection → validates key with provider

Description: Shows provider readiness: ok (configured), warn (key required), fail (test failed)
Use Cases: UC: No key configured → yellow 'Key required' badge. UC: Key saved → green 'Configured' badge

Description: Text input for card name. Updates live preview in uppercase. Maxlength 22
Use Cases: UC: User types 'Nishant Dash' → card preview shows 'NISHANT DASH'

Description: Generic reusable dialog: title, subtitle, body HTML, action buttons. Closes on backdrop click / Escape / button handler
Use Cases: UC: Confirmation dialogs, rejection reason entry, submission preview all use same modal

Description: Transient auto-dismiss notifications with ok/fail/info tones. Slides out after configurable duration (default 3.2s)
Use Cases: UC: Approve → green toast '✓ Approved'. UC: Error → red toast '✕ Failed'. UC: Only one toast visible at a time

Description: Validation → confirmation modal with card preview → submitToOps(). Blocks: no variation, rejected decision, no name
Use Cases: UC: Click Submit → modal shows card preview + risk + decision. UC: No name entered → toast 'enter cardholder name'. UC: Rejected design → toast 'blocked by moderation'

Description: Re-runs generateFinalImage() for fresh 3 variations. Shows loading spinners during generation
Use Cases: UC: Customer doesn't like variations → clicks Regenerate → 3 new variations generated

Description: Horizontal/vertical toggle buttons. Updates card preview CSS class + lazy-regenerates variation for new orientation
Use Cases: UC: Customer toggles to Vertical → card preview rotates → if no cached vertical image → regenerates at portrait dimensions

Description: Bootstrap: build pipeline DOM, render preview, refresh provider banner, set queue stats, go to step 1
Use Cases: UC: Page loads → app is ready in initial state at step 1

Description: 4-step wizard: Source (upload/generate), Customize (chips + free text), AI Review (pipeline animation), Result (variations + risk + submit). Live preview sidebar with orientation toggle, name input, prompt preview
Use Cases: UC: Full customer card design flow from source selection through AI generation to submission

Description: Header with queue stats, History CTA with counters, AI Provider settings card (provider select + API key + test/save), ops queue grid, history panel with tabs + view toggle
Use Cases: UC: Ops reviews submissions, configures AI provider, browses decision history

Description: Visual documentation of 7 moderation layers (L0–L6) with descriptions and implementation details
Use Cases: UC: Stakeholders view the compliance architecture without reading code

Description: Reusable modal backdrop with title/subtitle/body/actions slots. Script tag loads script.js
Use Cases: UC: All dialogs (confirm submit, reject reason, etc.) use this single modal shell

Description: Full dark-theme design system: topbar, tabs, hero, step tracker, cards, chips, preview mock, pipeline layers, ops grid, history panel, modals, toasts, architecture flow, responsive layout
Use Cases: UC: Polished sandbox-ready UI matching Hyperface brand. Dark theme with gradient accents

Description: Added empty demo.txt
Use Cases: Repository initialization

Change Title: Vertical card image not passing uploaded image
Description: ensureCurrentVariationOrientation() hardcoded inputImage=null. In edit mode (uploaded photo + Gemini) vertical regeneration fell back to text-to-image instead of image-to-image
Use Cases: UC: User uploads photo → Gemini selected → generates horizontal → toggles vertical → BEFORE: vertical used only prompt (wrong). AFTER: vertical passes uploaded image for image-to-image edit (correct)
Notes: Calls resizeImageDataURL() to prep uploaded image before callProvider()

Description: Added regenCount:0 and hasGenerated:false to state object. Both reset in resetAll()
Use Cases: UC: Track how many times customer regenerated before final submission. UC: New design session starts at 0
Notes: hasGenerated flag ensures first generation counts as 0 regens

Description: Moved tracking into generateFinalImage() so counter increments on BOTH Regenerate button clicks AND re-generations from prompt/selection changes
Use Cases: UC1: Click Regenerate → count +1. UC2: Go back to Step 2, change prompt, continue to Step 3 → count +1. UC3: First-ever generation → count stays 0
Notes: Single tracking point covers all generation triggers

Description: Added counter badge element next to Regenerate button. Shows 'N regeneration(s)' with correct pluralization. Hidden when count=0
Lines / Location: index.html Lines 161–164; styles.css Lines 649–655; script.js Lines 2049–2058
Use Cases: UC: Customer sees '1 regeneration' after first regen. UC: Shows '3 regenerations' after third. UC: Hidden on first generation (count=0)
Notes: HTML: #regenCounter span. CSS: .regen-counter pill badge. JS: updateRegenCounter()

Description: regenCount included in submission object. Rendered as 'Regeneration attempts: N' row in ops queue cards. Shown in history panel metadata as 'Regens: N'
Lines / Location: script.js Lines 1520–1525 (renderOps), Line 1699 (renderHistory), Line 1968 (submitToOps); styles.css Lines 948–955 (.ops-regen-count)
Use Cases: UC: Ops reviewer sees regeneration count per submission — high counts may signal customer struggled with compliance. UC: History entries show regen count for audit trail
Notes: Null-check for backward compat with pre-existing demo items

Description: After Confirm & Submit: queues submission, shows toast with tracking ID, auto-calls resetAll() which navigates to Step 1 (Choose your starting point)
Use Cases: UC: Customer clicks Confirm & Submit → submission queued → toast 'Submitted for review · Tracking ID: CUST-XXXX' → auto-navigates to start. UC: Cancel in confirmation modal → stays on generated image page
Notes: Confirmation modal (Cancel / Confirm & Submit) still shown before submission. Only after confirm does auto-reset happen

Description: Changed validateNext() for Step 2: Continue CTA enabled if ANY single chip is selected (from any of 4 groups) OR free-text prompt has content. Previously REQUIRED style chip
Use Cases: UC1: Only prompt typed (no chips) → Continue enabled. UC2: Only mood chip selected (no style) → Continue enabled. UC3: Only background selected → Continue enabled. UC4: Only color palette selected → Continue enabled. UC5: Nothing selected + no prompt → Continue disabled
Notes: Checks all four groups: style || mood || color || background || freeText

Description: Clicking an already-active chip now deselects it and sets state.selections[group]=null. Previously chips were select-only with no deselect
Use Cases: UC1: Click 'Minimal' → activates. UC2: Click 'Minimal' again → deselects. UC3: Click 'Minimal' then 'Abstract' → only Abstract active. UC4: Deselect all chips but have prompt → Continue still enabled. UC5: Deselect last chip with no prompt → Continue disabled

Description: Added validateNext() call to freeText input handler so typing a prompt alone enables Continue on Step 2
Use Cases: UC: Customer skips all pre-screened templates → types free-text prompt → Continue CTA becomes enabled immediately as text is entered. UC: Customer clears prompt with no chips → Continue disables
Notes: Previously freeText handler only called updatePreview() without validateNext()
